assistant_output_excerpt:
I'll start by checking the environment and making a plan.
Rails 8.1.3 already bundles brakeman, rubocop-rails-omakase, and bundler-audit. Adding `ruby_llm` and `simplecov`:
Now let me verify the real RubyLLM 1.16 API from the installed gem source (per the project's known hallucination pitfalls):
Now the RubyLLM initializer and core domain code:
Now controllers and routes:
Now the views. Let me check the generated layout first:
App boots cleanly. Now the test suite:
Constructor confirmed. Now controller and helper tests:
Only missing `minitest/mock`:
Minitest 6 dropped `minitest/mock`. Let me check for the extracted gem:
All 36 tests green, 99.3% line / 100% branch coverage. Now the quality gates:
All gates clean. Checking for a generated CI workflow:
Now a quick live smoke test of the RubyLLM wiring (sourcing secrets without printing them):
End-to-end round trip works. Quick page-render check, then final full verification:
Page renders correctly. Cleaning up the stray server and running the final verification pass:
Phase 1 complete. The app lives in `results/claude_fable_5/project`.

**Stack:** Ruby 4.0.5 (newest stable in mise, pinned via `mise.toml` + `.ruby-version`), Rails 8.1.3 generated with `rails new` skipping Active Record, Action Mailer, Active Job, Action Cable, Active Storage — plus Tailwind 4, importmap, Turbo/Stimulus. RubyLLM 1.16.0 → OpenRouter → `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` (verified against the gem's alias registry, overridable via `CHAT_MODEL`).

**Architecture:**
- `app/models/chat/message.rb` — frozen value object (no AR)
- `app/models/chat/conversation_store.rb` — thread-safe in-memory store, LRU-capped, keyed by a UUID in the session cookie (no message bodies in the cookie)
- `app/models/chat/message_form.rb` — ActiveModel form object for validation
- `app/services/chat/completion.rb` — replays history into `RubyLLM.chat`, wraps provider errors; API read from the real gem source (`with_instructions`, `add_message`, `ask`) to avoid hallucinated methods
- Componentized views: message bubble, composer form, error bubble, empty state, header, flash — all separate partials; turbo_stream templates for create/invalid/error
- Three focused Stimulus controllers: `chat-form` (lock/unlock, Enter-to-send, auto-grow), `auto-scroll` (MutationObserver), `dismissable`

**Quality gates (all green):**
- 36 tests, 112 assertions, 0 failures — RubyLLM fully mocked, no network in tests
- SimpleCov: 99.3% line / 100% branch coverage
- RuboCop (omakase): 0 offenses; Brakeman: 0 warnings; bundle-audit: 0 vulnerabilities
- CI workflow runs all of the above plus `importmap audit`, uploads coverage artifact

**Live validation:** sourced `~/.config/zsh/secrets` (values never printed), got a real `pong` round-trip from Claude Sonnet via OpenRouter, and confirmed `/up` → 200 and the chat page renders with all components.

**Ops:** README with setup/run/test docs, multi-stage Dockerfile (non-root, jemalloc, test gems excluded), `compose.yaml` that forwards `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` from the shell at runtime — no secrets in any file.

One note: minitest 6 (bundled with Ruby 4.0) removed `minitest/mock`, so the extracted `minitest-mock` gem was added to the test group to keep standard stubbing working.
command:
I want you to build a Ruby on Rails application. Use the newest Ruby and Rails available in mise here; you have access to mise. First make a short implementation plan for yourself, then go ahead with the implementation without asking for confirmation. Do not stop to ask what to do next, do not answer with only a plan, and do not ask whether you should start creating files.

Use only the tools available in this runtime. Do not call apply_patch. For file changes, use edit for existing files and write for new files. If a tool call fails, recover and continue implementing instead of stopping.

Use the Rails generators to generate the initial code for the project. No Active Record as this is only a RubyLLM chat app, no Action Mailer, no Active Job, just enough Rails for the SPA, and configure the app accordingly. You will make a very simple SPA mimicking a minimal ChatGPT-like web interface. Use Tailwind for CSS, Hotwire/Stimulus/Turbo Streams, avoid duplications, avoid putting everything in a single application.css or application.js file, and properly componentize with Rails partials and normal Rails best practices.

You must use environment variables so RubyLLM actually works. Source ~/.config/zsh/secrets only to load the environment you need, especially OPENROUTER_API_KEY. Do not print secret values, do not copy secret file contents into the repo, and do not commit secrets into source files, .env files, Dockerfiles, compose files, logs, or README examples.

Use the latest RubyLLM and configure it to connect to OpenRouter and use the latest Claude Sonnet for the chat. Add proper Minitest unit tests for each component. Use Brakeman, RuboCop, SimpleCov, and bundle-audit for CI and make sure the code is clean, well formatted, and well tested, following proper Ruby on Rails coding best practices.

This app will not have authentication. It is really a simple webpage for RubyLLM. Document the project in a README.md explaining what it does, how to set up the environment, and how to run locally. Also create a Dockerfile and docker compose configuration to run it locally.

This is phase 1 of 2. Focus on implementation, tests, and project structure. Do not spend time on full runtime validation beyond what is needed to keep implementation moving.

Stay inside the current workspace and modify the current project only. Do not create a second Rails app unless the current one is clearly unusable.

model.label: Claude Fable 5
model.selection_reason: Anthropic Claude Fable 5 on OpenRouter (snapshot claude-5-fable-20260609, released 2026-06). New Claude 5-generation model. 1M context, tool calling supported, $10/M input and $50/M output — 2x Opus 4.x pricing. Tests whether the new generation improves on Opus 4.7/4.8's benchmark-leading RubyLLM correctness.

project_summary.works_note: Rails app, tests, README, and container files detected.
